task_completion.description: Load the CT imaging series into InVesalius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius 3 interface is visible with the CT scan loaded, showing axial, coronal, and sagittal views of a human head/skull.

task_completion.description: Create a bone segmentation mask using appropriate thresholds
task_completion.visual_evidence: A segmentation mask (colored overlay, typically green) is visible on the 2D slices, isolating the bone structures. The threshold panel may be visible with a lower bound around 226 HU.
The 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a 3D reconstructed model of the skull.

task_completion.description: Place at least 12 linear measurements following a bilateral protocol
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface shows multiple linear measurement annotations (lines with distance values in mm) on the 3D model or 2D slices. Several measurements should be visible to indicate the required 12 measurements (bilateral pairs and midline).

task_completion.description: Export the STL, save 5 orientation screenshots, and save the InVesalius project
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of saving or the presence of the required files (`skull_model.stl`, `asymmetry_analysis.inv3`, and the 5 orientation PNGs: anterior, left/right lateral, superior, posterior) in the `/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study` directory, visible via a file manager, terminal `ls` command, or save dialogs.

integrity.description: Agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the InVesalius 3 GUI to load data, create masks, generate surfaces, and place measurements, rather than using command-line scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Measurements were placed using the application's native tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Measurements are created using the built-in measurement tools in InVesalius, visible as native UI elements, not edited into screenshots.

integrity.description: Files were exported genuinely rather than mocked
integrity.visual_evidence: Files are exported using InVesalius save/export dialogs or standard OS screenshot tools, rather than creating empty or dummy files via terminal commands like 'touch'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius 3 to process a CT scan. The bone mask threshold should be around 226 HU. The agent must generate a 3D surface and place at least 12 linear measurements (all >= 10 mm). The final output must include 'skull_model.stl', 'asymmetry_analysis.inv3', and 5 screenshots ('anterior_view.png', 'left_lateral.png', 'right_lateral.png', 'superior_view.png', 'posterior_view.png') saved in '/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study'.
